input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android 15+ / API 35), edge-to-edge display is enforced by default, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was deprecated. The recommended approach now is to use keyboard insets handling.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer about how to handle this in .NET 10.


In .NET 10, the shift to edge-to-edge layouts means I need to handle keyboard insets directly rather than relying on the deprecated resize approach—using `WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows` or the newer `AndroidX` insets API to listen for keyboard changes and adjust padding dynamically.
